title: Phenomenon 3
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Phenomenon 3: "Antigravity"}}{{VoidedTheme}} {{Phenomenon Class|effectiveness=5|frequency=5|danger=3}}
== Overview == '''Phenomenon 3,''' also known as '''Antigravity''', is the largely theoretical and poorly understood anomalous force responsible for manipulating gravity within most of the Backrooms to match that of the [[The Frontrooms|'''Frontrooms''']] (9.807 m/s²) despite the wildly varying (and in most cases infinite) mass of most '''[[Level List|Levels]]''', defying the known laws of gravity. While this concept was previously considered a side effect of the Backrooms' non-Euclidean nature, extensive research of static megastructures that should by all means be unable to support their own weight has warranted the classification of '''Antigravity''' as its own phenomena. {{Blurimage|file=LobbyDeeperRegions Map.png|caption=A 3D recreation of gravity-defying level structure. Data gathered with sonar.}} Backrooms researchers were presented with quite the challenge when initially attempting to explain these anomalies and exceptions to known science. Eventually the term "'''Antigravity'''" was coined as a temporary explanation, being adopted by the general public for use in casual conversations, similar to the term "anti-matter"Antimatter is speculated to be invisible and undetectable except through algorithmic discrepancies on a planetary scale. used to explain cosmic gravitational anomalies in the [[The Frontrooms|'''Frontrooms''']]. As of now, it is unknown exactly how '''Antigravity''' functions or what 'purpose' it serves besides closely replicating the conditions of the '''Frontrooms'''. However, most researchers can agree that '''Antigravity''' is its own force and, despite all appearances, normal gravity is completely absent from the Backrooms. '''Antigravity''' merely creates the illusion of regular gravity with a standard 'downward' force towards the perceived 'floor' of each Level (such as '''[[Level 0]]''''s carpet or '''[[Level 1]]''''s asphalt), but the other laws of gravity that would normally condense or collapse said Levels do not apply. The all-encompassing theoretical nature of '''Antigravity''' means it is present by default in every '''[[Level List|Level]]''', unless stated otherwise. It, along with the non-Euclidean nature of many Levels, may also account for the consistent failure of navigation devices such as compasses, which normally rely on the Earth's magnetic field. Others have theorized that '''Antigravity''' conforms to the wanderer's perception; that is, if one believes they are in an ordinary room, the gravitational force of the environment will match that, versus a region such as [[Level 24|'''Level 24''']] having gravitational forces suited to its extraterrestrial environment (similar to the idea of quantum particles switching states when under direct observation). This idea has yet to be disproven, given that the megastructural scale of most Levels has only been documented by drones thus far, and not by human eyes. Of course, if this ''were'' the case, any wanderer who personally observed enough of a Level's structure at once to understand the true scale of it would be immediately killed by the subsequent adjustment of '''Antigravity''' destroying said Level. If '''Antigravity''' were suddenly replaced by gravity native to the '''[[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]]''', the whole of the Backrooms would likely collapse on itself instantly (and infinitely), creating what one expert compared to a "reverse big bang" in which all matter in the realm would attempt to concentrate into a single point. No life could possibly survive this theoretical occurrence. '''Antigravity''' may also be responsible for previously unexplained spatial anomalies, such as '''[[The Void|Void Objects]]''' being suspended by nothing or a consistent Backrooms-wide failure of falling humans to reach terminal velocity despite falling for extended periods of time, such as while no-clipping. == Readjustment Areas == {{Blurimage|file=ReOrientation.png|caption=A Readjustment Zone photographed from below.}} The most definite proof of the existence of '''Antigravity''', and what prompted its research and subsequent categorization, are '''Readjustment Areas'''. '''Readjustment Areas''' are extremely rare anomalous zones with bizarre geometry, manifesting across many levels, but most commonly 'indoors' ones, in which gravity behaves unnaturally. Most wanderers have encountered at least one '''Readjustment Area''' while in the backrooms, though they may not have realized it at the time. '''Readjustment Areas''' are characterized by structural impossibilities or the adornment of level decor on 'incorrect' surfaces (e.g. fluorescent ceiling lights embedded into the walls, or puddles of water in the ceiling) and the switching of gravity to match the orientation of said geometric anomalies, allowing experienced travelers to do otherwise impossible feats like walking on walls. While relatively uncommon, '''Readjustment Areas''' have been reported in many levels, primarily by wanderers who traveled an excessive distance in said level without '''[[The Void|no clipping]]'''. '''Readjustment Areas''' can be heavily disorienting and even dangerous. One wrong step could realign gravity from the ceiling to the floor, causing grievous injuries depending on the height of the room (or even death, should a wanderer become suddenly reoriented towards the sky instead of the ground in an outdoor Level). == Discovery == '''Phenomenon 3''' has existed in the Backrooms for as long as we know of, though its formal documentation was only created recently due to ideological overlaps with other Phenomena. Experts still debate the specifics of '''Phenomenon 3''' due to its irrational nature and Backrooms-wide ramifications. {{Collapse|num=3|initial='''> Open Author Information'''|final='''> Close Author Information'''|body= Page written by [[User:SlenderTales|SlenderTales]].
Images from "Backrooms Rec" game and "Deepest Regions of the Backrooms" YouTube video. {{User:SlenderTales/MyPages}}}}
